title: cornus nuttallii
intro: Cornus nuttallii, also known as Pacific dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, fever, and other infections. The bark and roots of the Pacific dogwood contain a compound called berberine, which has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Pacific dogwood can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, although its preparation and dosage should be done under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
